title: Marbella Luxury Catamaran Charter Group: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Planning a group getaway on the Costa del Sol? A luxury catamaran charter in Marbella offers an unforgettable day on the Mediterranean, with prices typically starting from around EUR 3,000 for a full-day private trip. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators so you can compare options and secure the ideal vessel for your party.

Where to set off in Marbella

Marbella’s coastline is dotted with excellent marinas. Puerto Banús is the most famous, packed with superyachts and high-end boutiques. Nearby, the Real Club Náutico de Marbella offers a more traditional base, while the port of Estepona, just west, provides a relaxed alternative. For a longer cruise, head east to Cala de Mijas or the secluded Cabopino harbour. The Bay of Marbella is sheltered and perfect for first-time sailors.

Which boat to rent

  1. Luxury Catamaran, ideal for groups wanting stability and space; 12 guests; from EUR 4,500 per day.
  2. Motor Yacht (e.g. Maiora 26m), perfect for speed and style; 10, 14 guests; from EUR 6,000 per day. To see detailed specs, check this luxury yacht on BoatHire24.
  3. Sailing Monohull, authentic experience; 6, 8 guests; from EUR 2,500 per day.
  4. RIB or Small Powerboat, best for active water sports; 4, 6 guests; from EUR 800 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Group catamaran charters range from EUR 3,000 to EUR 8,000 for a full day, depending on size and season. Shared tours are also available at around EUR 150 per person, often including lunch and drinks. A private crewed catamaran for 12 guests typically falls between EUR 4,000 and EUR 6,000 in high season. For a more intimate gathering, a half-day motorboat hire can cost as little as EUR 600. You can rent a boat that matches your group’s size and budget through verified local operators.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

For most bareboat charters (no skipper), a Spanish navigation licence is required, but skippered vessels need no licence from you. Many luxury catamarans come with a professional skipper and crew, making the trip completely hassle‑free. If you prefer to take the helm yourself, the operator will confirm exact licensing requirements; Spanish law generally requires a ‘Patrón de Navegación Básica’ for boats up to 8 metres and within 5 nautical miles of a port. High season (June to September) brings the Levante wind, which can stiffen in the afternoon, so an experienced skipper is advisable.

Booking through BoatHire24 ensures the operator provides clear guidance on local regulations, so you can focus on enjoying the coastline.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 150 per person shared to EUR 8,000 private full-day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) to full day (8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 4, 14 depending on boat type
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can we charter a catamaran without a licence in Marbella?

Yes, if you hire a skippered catamaran you do not need any licence and can relax while the crew handles the sailing.

What is included in a typical luxury catamaran charter?

Most charters include a professional skipper, fuel, soft drinks, and often a light lunch; premium packages add a hostess, gourmet catering, and water toys.

How far can we sail from Marbella in a day?

A typical excursion covers the coast between Benalmádena and Estepona, about 20 nautical miles, with stops for swimming and lunch at anchor.

If you own a charter vessel, consider listing it on the platform to reach thousands of travellers planning trips in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
